title: elymus pauciflorus subsp. subsecundus
intro: Elymus pauciflorus subsp. subsecundus is a type of wild grass, also known as blue wildrye, which is native to North America and is commonly found in the western regions of the continent. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Elymus pauciflorus subsp. subsecundus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, Elymus pauciflorus subsp. subsecundus is often used as a tea or tincture, and it can also be dried and used as a medicinal powder.
